content.rendered:
This volume concludes the “Meeting Across the River” trilogy, a series within the Cover Me series collecting the hundreds of songs Bruce Springsteen has collaborated on with other artists.

This one’s a bit rag-tag, containing a handful of my earliest essays (some of which, it’s painfully apparent to me with distance, were rather phoned in to fill space between my “major” essays), but also ten brand new ones–more new writing than in any Bookshelf Collection volume to date.

Most of the new entries cover new studio collaborations from the last few years, but there are a few like “It’s Been a Long Time” and “Sun City’ that would have been unforgivable of me to leave out of any comprehensive Springsteen anthology.

Given how prolific Bruce has been lately, I wouldn’t be surprised to find the need for an appendix volume when this project is complete in 2028, but for now this closes the book (pun intended) on my Meeting Across the River sub-blog.

Thanks for reading over the years. I hope you enjoy this final collection.

excerpt.rendered: This volume concludes the “Meeting Across the River” trilogy, a series within the Cover Me series collecting the hundreds of songs Bruce Springsteen has collaborated on…

aioseo_breadcrumb: Home » It Takes Two » Preface

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Jimmy Eat World: Take ‘Em as They Come
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Jimmy Eat World absolutely nails it with their cover of this gem from Tracks. It's one of the best Springsteen covers out there, if you can find it.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: December 16
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: It's the debut of "Brothers Under the Bridge," the release of "The Wrestler," and the last "Crush on You" for 28 years as Bruce's tours start to wind down for the year.

jetpack-related-posts.title: I Can’t Stand Up For Falling Down
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce Springsteen and Elvis Costello team up on a Sam and Dave classic made famous by Elvis in 1980.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Cry to Me
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: During the Born to Run Tour, Bruce worked up a simmering, slow burn of a cover of Solomon Burke's 1962 classic.

jetpack-related-posts.title: October 7
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce debuted (and re-debuted, and re-debuted) "You Can Look (But You Better Not Touch)" on this date. Plus: Bruce recalls his first-ever show in Africa, is interviewed on-stage at The New Yorker Festival, and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Bright Lights, Big City
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: At the opening show of The Bruce Springsteen Band's month-long residency at The Back Door in February 1972, Southside Johnny takes the spotlight when Bruce and the band cover Jimmy Reed's classic.
